meta.purpose: Encode world-class judgment for programmatic video animation with Motion Canvas: scenes, signals, layout, camera rhythm, explainers, and code maintainability.
meta.load_condition: Load when the task involves Motion Canvas, programmatic video generation, technical explainer videos, or converting code architecture into visual animations.

axioms.statement: A Motion Canvas scene should express one argument or one visual transformation.
axioms.why: Overloaded scenes become hard to read, review, and reuse.

axioms.statement: Animation code must remain readable as a storyboard.
axioms.why: If timing logic is scattered, the video cannot be directed.

axioms.statement: Technical animations should prove an idea visually, not merely decorate narration.
axioms.why: The viewer should understand the logic even with the sound muted.

axioms.statement: Repeated visual motifs create cognitive continuity.
axioms.why: Explainers become memorable when transformations reuse visual grammar.

ontology.name: scene
ontology.definition: A self-contained motion unit with one communication purpose.

ontology.name: signal
ontology.definition: A reactive value that drives animation state over time.

ontology.name: layout
ontology.definition: A composition structure that determines spatial hierarchy.

ontology.name: beat
ontology.definition: A time segment where one idea changes visually.

core_structure.input: brief, audience, surface, constraints, brand tone, implementation environment
core_structure.judgment: attention path, timing, hierarchy, motion grammar, technical feasibility, review criteria
core_structure.output: motion direction, implementation guidance, critique, and measurable acceptance criteria

stances.stance: Prefer composable scene components and named animation beats over clever but opaque code.
Use motion to reveal cause and effect, not just make diagrams move.
